Credit Conditions Signal
← Back to dashboard

Credit Conditions Signal

Credit conditions composite: HY/BBB spreads and equity volatility.

As of 2036-10-01

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a neutral Credit_Regime of 0.30 and a Z_VIX spike to 2.25, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • The signal remains in a Neutral regime but exhibits a sharp upward inflection in raw stress levels, currently testing the tightening threshold. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • The composite index identifies tightening conditions (risk-off) when the value exceeds 0.75. (1)
  • Neutral funding environments are defined by index values between -0.75 and 0.75. (1)
  • The model uses Z-scores for HY_OAS, BBB_OAS, and VIX to distinguish between funding-led and sentiment-led stress. (1)
  • Latest observation date: 2026-03-31. (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the VIX component (Z_VIX: 2.25), indicating sentiment-driven stress over credit-specific funding pressure. (1)
  • High-yield and BBB spreads remain relatively stable near their historical means (Z_HY: -0.01; Z_BBB: -0.11), creating a divergence with equity volatility. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: Index falls below -0.75, signalling a return to compressed risk premia. (1)
  • Persistence: The signal has occupied the Neutral zone since mid-2024 but is currently at its most volatile state in 24 months. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Funding remains available despite rising equity market uncertainty.
  • Upside risk: Tightening regime if credit spreads gap wider to align with current VIX levels.
  • Downside risk: Easing regime if VIX mean-reverts while corporate spreads remain subdued.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks), as VIX-led spikes often precede or fade faster than structural credit cycles.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; the proximity to the 0.75 threshold suggests high sensitivity to upcoming data.

Macro Relevance:

  • Dimension: Financial liquidity and sentiment-based risk premia.
  • Mechanism: Rising VIX implies an increase in the cost of hedging and equity-risk aversion, though physical credit flow (OAS) has not yet constricted.
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically acts as a lead indicator for risk-asset drawdowns when spreads and volatility align.

Regime Context:

  • The regime is persistent Neutral but currently weakening (moving toward Tightening).
  • Direction of change: Strengthening stress (transitioning from easing toward the upper neutral boundary).

Model Limitations:

  • EMA(3) smoothing may lag sudden volatility shocks. (1)
  • VIX includes non-credit variance which may overstate systemic funding stress. (1)

Data & References:

  • Credit Conditions Signal (2026-03-31). (1)
  • Influential data: VIX (27.29), Credit_Conditions_Raw (0.71). (1)
  • Public datasets: FRED STLFSI4 (Financial Stress) and M2 Money Supply would improve liquidity context.

Credit Conditions Chart

Credit spreads and volatility combined into a single financial conditions index.
